text:
All checks pass. Summary:

### Changes applied

**`rust/crates/astra-test-harness/src/classify.rs`**

| Fix | What changed |
|---|---|
| **Generic pattern tightened** | `has_generic_dont_have` now requires `"available" OR "in my"` after "don't have a ... tool" — blocks casual false positives like *"I don't have a good tool for this"*. |
| **`generic_tool_phrase_does_not_false_positive` test added** | Explicitly tests that `"don't have a good tool for this task"` does **not** trigger `ToolUnavailable`. |
| **Duplicate `tool_unavailable_generic_tool_name` removed** | Stale older definition cleaned up. |

**`rust/crates/runtime/src/tool_registry/registry.rs`**

- `>=` → `>` in assertion (proves dynamic tools added, not just not-decreased)
- New `quality_path_conversational_with_recent_tools_runs_dynamic` test (symmetric to the existing `report_ctx` test)
- Import alphabetical ordering only (cosmetic)

**`rust/crates/runtime/src/tool_registry/scoring.rs`**

- `pre_filter_dynamic_core` now checks `recent_tools.is_empty()` before short-circuiting — fixes the parallel code path that the previous commit only patched in `registry.rs`

**Result**: 13/13 classify tests pass, 130/130 registry tests pass.

[Turn context: tool_calls: 3 | verification: ✅ cargo test -p astra-test-harness --lib classify::tests — 13 passed]
